meta.name: Tonkatsu (Japanese Breaded Pork Cutlet)
meta.subtitle: Crispy panko-crusted pork cutlet with shredded cabbage + tonkatsu-style sauce

source.name: ssa-core
notes: Deterministic: includes pan-fry default and oven/air-fryer adaptations.
Includes oil safety, doneness targets, and make-ahead notes.

yield.notes: Serves 4 with cabbage + sauce; scale as needed.

signals.notes: Breaded raw cutlets freeze well. Fried cutlets re-crisp best in oven/air fryer.

tasteProfile.balanceNotes: Crisp savory pork + tangy-sweet tonkatsu sauce + fresh cabbage.

notes: For gluten-free: use GF flour + GF panko/cracker crumbs and verify sauce ingredients.
For egg-free: use aquafaba or buttermilk substitute for dredge (results vary).

ingredients.label: Boneless pork loin chops (or pork cutlets), 1/2–3/4 inch thick
ingredients.notes: About 4 pieces (5–6 oz each). Pork tenderloin slices also work.

ingredients.label: Panko breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: About 2 cups; use fresh, dry panko for best crunch.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (canola, peanut, or sunflower) for frying
ingredients.notes: Depth depends on pan; aim for 1/2–3/4 inch oil for shallow-fry.

ingredients.label: Green cabbage, very finely shredded
ingredients.notes: Classic tonkatsu side; soak in cold water briefly for extra crispness.

ingredients.label: Tonkatsu sauce (store-bought)
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup; serve for dipping or drizzling.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Optional; adds depth to DIY sauce.

ingredients.label: Sugar
ingredients.notes: About 2 tsp; adjust to taste for DIY sauce.

steps.title: Prep and season the pork
instructions: Pat pork dry. If chops have a fat cap or connective strip, make a few small snips along the edge to prevent curling.
If thicker than ~3/4 inch, gently pound to an even thickness (about 1/2–3/4 inch).
Season both sides with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Set up dredging station
instructions: Place flour in a shallow dish.
Beat eggs in a second shallow dish.
Place panko in a third dish; fluff with fingers for an airy crust.
checks: Three stations ready: flour → egg → panko

steps.title: Bread the cutlets
instructions: Dredge each cutlet in flour, shaking off excess.
Dip into beaten egg, letting excess drip off.
Press into panko firmly so crumbs adhere; coat both sides and edges.
Place breaded cutlets on a rack or plate while oil heats.

steps.title: Heat oil for shallow-frying
instructions: Add oil to a heavy skillet/dutch oven to reach 1/2–3/4 inch depth.
Heat to about 340–350°F (170–175°C). Use a thermometer if possible.
If no thermometer: drop in a few panko crumbs—should sizzle immediately and turn light golden within ~15–20 seconds.

steps.title: Fry until golden and cooked through
instructions: Fry 1–2 cutlets at a time to avoid cooling the oil.
Cook ~3–4 minutes per side (depending on thickness) until deep golden brown.
Transfer to a wire rack to drain (avoid paper towels if you want maximum crispness).
Check doneness: pork should reach 145°F (63°C) in the thickest part, then rest 3 minutes.

steps.title: Optional: quick tonkatsu-style sauce
instructions: If not using store-bought sauce, whisk ketchup + Worcestershire + sugar.
Optional: add soy sauce, garlic powder, and ginger powder to taste.
Adjust sweetness/tang: add sugar for sweeter, Worcestershire for more tang.

steps.title: Slice and serve
instructions: Slice cutlets into strips (classic presentation).
Serve with shredded cabbage and tonkatsu sauce for dipping/drizzling.
Optional: add lemon wedge, rice, and miso soup for a full set meal.

pairings.notes: Tonkatsu is rich and crispy; pair with light soup, pickles, and fresh cabbage.

notes: Store cutlets on paper towel-lined container to reduce sogginess.
Re-crisp in 375°F oven 8–12 minutes on a rack, or air-fryer 6–8 minutes.
Best method: freeze breaded raw cutlets on a tray, then bag.
Cook from frozen: air fryer 375°F ~14–18 minutes, flipping halfway; or oven 400°F ~18–25 minutes, until 145°F internal.

planner.weeknightNotes: Bread earlier in the day; fry right before serving. Or air-fry for lower mess.

notes: Oil safety: never fill pan more than halfway with oil; keep children/pets away; do not add wet food to hot oil.
For extra crispness: rest breaded cutlets 5–10 minutes before frying so coating hydrates and adheres better.
Avoid overcrowding—oil temp drops cause greasy crust.
Preheat air fryer to 375°F (190°C). Spray cutlets lightly with oil.
Cook 10–14 minutes (fresh) flipping halfway, until golden and 145°F internal.
For deeper color, mist with oil again after flipping.
Toast panko on a sheet pan at 350°F for 3–5 minutes until pale golden (optional but helps browning).
Bake breaded cutlets on a rack at 425°F (220°C) for 15–20 minutes, flipping once, until 145°F internal.
Use chicken cutlets/thigh cutlets. Fry similarly; cook to 165°F (74°C) internal.
Sauce and cabbage pair the same.

summary: Crispy Japanese panko-breaded pork cutlets (tonkatsu) served with shredded cabbage and tangy-sweet sauce. Includes shallow-fry, oven, and air-fryer options plus doneness targets.

foodSafety: Use a thermometer for doneness: pork 145°F (63°C) then rest 3 minutes.
Hot oil burns fast—use long tongs and avoid overcrowding.
Keep breaded cutlets dry; moisture causes oil splatter.
allergenNotes: Contains gluten (panko/flour) and egg; sauce may include soy/gluten.
Use GF alternatives and verify labels if needed.
